channel.title: SteadyDevs Blog - Legacy .NET Systems Insights
channel.description: Expert insights on maintaining, stabilizing, and improving legacy .NET systems. Practical tips and real-world case studies from 11+ years of production experience.

item.title: Cloud Migration vs On-Premise Modernization for .NET Systems
item.description: Considering cloud migration for your legacy .NET system? Learn when moving to the cloud makes sense versus modernizing on-premise, based on real-world experience and practical cost considerations.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Your POS Vendor Won't Support Customizations. Now What?
item.description: Your POS vendor refuses to support customizations or integrate with your systems. You're stuck between losing warranty support and losing business efficiency. Here's how to move forward without burning bridges.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Your Developer Left. Now What? (Emergency Recovery Guide)
item.description: Your only developer quit, disappeared, or can't be reached. Your legacy system is running but nobody knows how it works. Here's your immediate action plan for recovering from developer departure without destroying the business.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The E-Invoice Mandate: What Actually Happened to Businesses That Missed the Deadline
item.description: Real stories from Malaysian businesses that missed e-Invoice deadlines. What penalties were enforced, what compliance looks like now, and practical steps if you're still not ready.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The Excel Trap: When Spreadsheets Cost More Than Custom Software
item.description: Excel is free, flexible, and familiar. So why do businesses end up paying more when relying on spreadsheets instead of proper software? Here's what the hidden costs actually look like.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Your Developer Says the System Is "Too Old to Fix." Is That Actually True?
item.description: When a developer tells you your legacy system is beyond repair, is it really unfixable or just unfamiliar? Learn how to evaluate the claim and determine your real options.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The 3-Month System Stabilization Sprint: What Actually Happens
item.description: A detailed breakdown of what happens during a focused 3-month system stabilization engagement—real deliverables, realistic expectations, and what success actually looks like.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: From Manual to Automated Invoicing: The Real Business Impact
item.description: Real case study: A Malaysian SME automated their invoicing process. Here's what actually changed—the time saved, errors eliminated, and unexpected benefits that made the investment worthwhile.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: What Happens to Your Business When the Developer Who Knows Your System Leaves
item.description: Most businesses don't realise how much critical knowledge lives in one person's head—until they're gone. Here's what actually happens, and how to protect yourself before it becomes a crisis.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: 5 Signs Your Business Is Ready for Automation (And What to Automate First)
item.description: Business automation isn't about replacing people—it's about stopping them from doing work that software should be doing. Here are 5 clear signs your business is ready, and what to automate first.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Manual Invoicing in Malaysian SMEs
item.description: Manual invoicing costs Malaysian SMEs more than you think. Discover the hidden expenses in time, errors, cash flow delays, and missed opportunities—and why automation is no longer optional.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: How We Saved a Legacy System: An Honest Assessment Framework
item.description: Not every legacy system can—or should—be saved. Here's the honest assessment framework I use to determine whether a system is worth fixing, and how we successfully rescued one that others had written off.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The Price of Ignoring That "Small Bug" in Your Production System
item.description: A small bug in production seems harmless—until it isn't. Real stories of minor issues that grew into major problems, and how to decide what's truly urgent versus what can wait.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: What Really Happens When You Delay Legacy System Maintenance
item.description: Delaying legacy system maintenance feels like saving money in the short term. Here's what actually happens—the costs that accumulate, the risks that grow, and when the bill comes due.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Is Your Legacy .NET System Ready for Malaysia's E-Invoice Mandate?
item.description: Malaysia's e-Invoice mandate is rolling out. If your business runs on a legacy .NET system, here's what you need to know about compliance, integration, and whether your current system can handle it.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: AI-Assisted Development Without the Code Chaos: Building a Pet Booking System
item.description: Can AI tools like Claude actually speed up development without creating unmaintainable code? Here's what happened when I used AI to build a complex booking system—and how to avoid the common pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: When to Refactor vs. Rewrite Your Legacy .NET System: A Practical Guide
item.description: Facing a difficult legacy .NET system? Learn when refactoring makes sense versus when a full rewrite is justified, based on 11 years of real-world experience with production systems.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: 5 Warning Signs Your Legacy .NET System Needs Professional Attention
item.description: Is your legacy .NET system showing signs of trouble? Learn the 5 critical warning signs that indicate it's time to seek professional help before small issues become major problems.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Should You Add Features to a Failing System? When New Features Make Things Worse
item.description: Your system is unstable, but the business wants new features. Do you add functionality on top of a shaky foundation, or fix the foundation first? Here's how to make that call.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Why Is Your POS System Getting Slower? (And How to Fix It Without Replacing Everything)
item.description: Your POS system used to be fast. Now every transaction takes longer, reports time out, and your staff is frustrated. Here's why this happens and how to fix it without buying a new system.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs
